creator: oleglelchuk@gmail.com
text: Because at first I didn't know how to fix compiler errors in the asl file that was decompiled from the original aml file, I loaded a distro of linux and after dumping the DSDT tables again, noticed that errors were easier to fix this time. I fixed the errors, then applied the changes that were described in the previous post, and used the resulting aml file to override the memory tables on FreeBSD 11 and 10.x. However, then I decided to struggle with fixing the errors that I got after compiling the original asl file on FreeBSD. I eventually fixed them, but the changes that I described in the previous post didn't make /dev/psm0 come to life this time. So, I applied the code from this site http://www.tonymacx86.com/threads/ps2-not-working-after-installing-dsdt.53498 and this time /dev/psm0 came to life. I have no idea why the asl file that I got on Linux was different from the one that I got on FreeBSD. Both OSes used the same version of iasl to decompile the aml file.
